name: ESOPHAGEAL TRAUMA
definition: SUBMUCOSAL DISSECTION = INTRAMURAL RUPTURE OF ESOPHAGUS = DISSECTING mucosal tear with dissecting hemorrhage into submucosa + involvement of venous plexus ESOPHAGEAL PERFORATION = ESOPHAGEAL RUPTURE
discriminating_features: well-defined focal filling defect = intramural hematoma simulating retained solid material
"double-barrel esophagus" = mucosal flap with submucosal distribution of gas / contrast
"mucosal stripe" sign = dissected mucosa floating within lumen
eccentric / concentric nonenhancing hyperattenuating mass within esophageal wall

general: well-defined focal filling defect = intramural hematoma simulating retained solid material
"double-barrel esophagus" = mucosal flap with submucosal distribution of gas / contrast
"mucosal stripe" sign = dissected mucosa floating within lumen
eccentric / concentric nonenhancing hyperattenuating mass within esophageal wall
CT: well-defined nonenhancing intramural esophageal mass of high attenuation
"V" sign of Naclerio = extrapleural air within lower mediastinum between parietal
delayed widening of the mediastinum ← mediastinitis
hydrothorax (after rupture into pleural cavity), usually unilateral on left side
confirmation with contrast study (90% of contrast esophagrams are positive)
extravasation of oral contrast material into mediastinum ± pleural space
focal extraluminal air collection at site of tear (92%; most useful sign)

clinical.Prognosis: progression to frank esophageal rupture / resolution within a few weeks
